expectations.text: Output classifies the task as Tier 1
expectations.evidence: Baseline does not use Tier framework. Says 'Tier-1 task' in REASONING freeform but does not use it as a routing classifier.

expectations.text: Output names plain Agent() in-process dispatch (no team_name) — preferably test-specialist or general-purpose subagent
expectations.evidence: MECHANISM: 'Inline execution by myself' — baseline keeps it inline rather than dispatching to a focused subagent. This is actually one tier LIGHTER than rocky's recommendation (Tier 0 instead of Tier 1).

expectations.text: Output explicitly states this is a one-shot dispatch / no tmux pane
expectations.evidence: Baseline says 'no parallelism' but does not articulate the dispatch shape in rocky's vocabulary.

expectations.text: Output explicitly states cross-model rule does not apply (per the mechanical-work exception)
expectations.evidence: Baseline does not invoke cross-model rule — no mention. Self-review only.

expectations.text: Output does NOT escalate to a swarm session, panes, or wave dispatch
expectations.evidence: Baseline stayed inline — definitely no escalation. Correct semantic decision.

claims.claim: Inline is the right call for a 3-test addition
claims.evidence: Baseline picked inline (one tier lighter than rocky's Tier 1 subagent recommendation). Either would work — neither is wrong, but rocky's choice offloads boilerplate context to the subagent.

suggestions.reason: The baseline result for Tier 1 reveals an interesting question: is sending to a focused subagent strictly better than doing it inline for a 3-test addition? The skill should explain WHY Tier 1 prefers a subagent over Opus inline — context offload, model fit, etc.

eval_feedback.overall: Useful — shows rocky's Tier 1 is not strictly necessary, just preferable for context efficiency.
